Nantes earns first trophy in 21 years in French Cup final  Nantes coach Antoine Kombouare has won the trophy twice as a player for Paris Saint-Germain, and twice as a coach with PSG in 2010 and now Nantes.

Subscribers onlyA small tribute to sexual passivity  In a society that values active, even hyperactive, sexuality, laziness during intimate relations is considered a turn-off. This is a mistake, explains 'Le Monde' columnist Maïa Mazaurette.

News analysis   Elsa Conesa Subscribers onlyThe French paradox of 'sad passion' as seen from abroad The doom and gloom that affects France puzzles some Anglophone press correspondents, who have a less critical view of our country. The origins of our obsession with decline are partly rooted in history.   6 min read

Feature   Ravi Pinto Subscribers onlyBehind India's government jobs frenzy and the generation of young people longing for a future A lack of job opportunities means that a whole generation of Indians is scrambling to get government roles. Many young people sacrifice several years preparing for entrance exams for unskilled positions, but only a fraction of the candidates manage to land a job.   6 min read

Investigation   Lionel Paillès  Subscribers onlyThe perfume industry braces for a shortage of raw materials Essential perfumery ingredients sometimes run out due to climatic or geopolitical reasons. It's a nightmare for the luxury industry, which is exploring alternative, ethical avenues to preserve its iconic perfume brands.   9 min read
